task_completion.description: Navigate to the target product and open Advanced Inventory settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Magento Admin product edit page for 'Classic Cotton T-Shirt' (SKU: TSHIRT-001) is visible, and the 'Advanced Inventory' modal or section is open.

task_completion.description: Set the Minimum Qty Allowed in Shopping Cart to 5
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Advanced Inventory settings, the 'Minimum Qty Allowed in Shopping Cart' input field contains the value '5'. The 'Use Config Settings' checkbox next to it should be unchecked.

task_completion.description: Set the Maximum Qty Allowed in Shopping Cart to 50
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Advanced Inventory settings, the 'Maximum Qty Allowed in Shopping Cart' input field contains the value '50'. The 'Use Config Settings' checkbox next to it should be unchecked.
In the Advanced Inventory settings, the 'Enable Qty Increments' dropdown is set to 'Yes'. The 'Use Config Settings' checkbox next to it should be unchecked.

task_completion.description: Set the Qty Increments amount to 5
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Advanced Inventory settings, the 'Qty Increments' input field contains the value '5'. The 'Use Config Settings' checkbox next to it should be unchecked.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Magento Admin web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Magento Admin panel, rather than a text editor or script.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or database queries to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows, SSH sessions, or database clients (like MySQL CLI) visible showing direct manipulation of the cataloginventory_stock_item table.

integrity.description: The changes were made to the specific product, not globally
integrity.visual_evidence: The settings are being modified on the product-specific 'Advanced Inventory' screen for SKU 'TSHIRT-001', rather than the global Stores > Configuration > Inventory page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target product is 'Classic Cotton T-Shirt' with SKU 'TSHIRT-001'. The required inventory settings are: Minimum Qty Allowed in Shopping Cart = 5, Maximum Qty Allowed in Shopping Cart = 50, Enable Qty Increments = Yes (1), and Qty Increments = 5. These settings are found in the 'Advanced Inventory' modal on the product edit page in the Magento Admin panel. For these fields to be edited, their respective 'Use Config Settings' checkboxes must be unchecked.
